messages.content:
I have two excel sheets.
Sheet Providers contains data of my contractors.
Column C contains the contractor company name,
Column D contains a Sheet Name,
Column F a contact name and
Column G the contacts email address all in the same row.
Occasionally, a contractor might not have a contact name or an email and these cells will be blank.

In my other Sheet Job Request, I want to be able to select in A2 the contacts name from a list, find the matching value in Column C in Sheet Providers then from the same row, automatically populate the relevant contact name into G2, the relevant email address into H2 and the relevant Sheet Name into I2  using formulas that will do this for each of the cells G2, H2 and I2 of Sheet Job Request.

Values in cells B2, C2, D2, E2 and F2 in Sheet Job Request, data will be entered manually.
When F2 in Sheet Job Request is entered, which will be a date, I want a VBA code that will open and select the Specific Sheet using the text value in I2 of Sheet Job Request but only if cells A2, B2, C2, D2 and E2 are not blank .then when the new sheet is opened and selected, find in it the next empty row and
populate the empty cell in Column B with the value from B2 in Sheet Job Request,
populate the empty cell in Column C of the same row with the value from F2 in Sheet Job Request,
populate the empty cell in Column F of the same row with the value from C2 in Sheet Job Request,
populate the empty cell in Column J of the same row with the value from E2 in Sheet Job Request.

When this has been completed, I want to return to Sheet Job Request, open a text document form this location G:\Shared drives\Swan School Site Premises\PREMISES MANAGEMENT\SERVICE PROVIDERS\Request0.txt and paste the values of A2, B2, C2, D2 and E2 into the text document each value on a different line.

As an AI language model, I cannot do manual work but I can provide the logical steps that can be followed to achieve your requirements in Excel VBA.

Step 1: Set up a drop-down list in cell A2 of Sheet Job Request to allow users to select the contact name from Sheet Providers.
Step 2: Use the VLOOKUP function to find the matching value in Column C of Sheet Providers based on the selected contact name in A2 of Sheet Job Request. Use this formula in G2 of Sheet Job Request: =IF(A2<>"",VLOOKUP(A2,Providers!C:G,4,FALSE),""). This will return the contact email address in the same row from Column G of Sheet Providers.
Step 3: Use this formula in H2 of Sheet Job Request to return the contact email address: =IF(A2<>"",VLOOKUP(A2,Providers!C:G,5,FALSE),"").
Step 4: Use this formula in I2 of Sheet Job Request to return the sheet name: =IF(A2<>"",VLOOKUP(A2,Providers!C:D,2,FALSE),"").
Step 5: Create a button or use a worksheet_change event to trigger the macro when F2 is entered in Sheet Job Request.
Step 6: In the VBA code, check if all the required cells are not blank. Use the following code:

If Range("A2") = "" Or Range("B2") = "" Or Range("C2") = "" Or Range("D2") = "" Or Range("E2") = "" Then
MsgBox "Please fill in all the required fields.", vbExclamation, "Missing Information"
Exit Sub
End If

Step 7: Use the following code to open and select the sheet from the value in I2:

Dim ws As Worksheet
Set ws = Worksheets(Range("I2").Value)
ws.Select

Step 8: Use the following code to find the next empty row in the selected sheet and populate the data:

Dim lastrow As Long
lastrow = ws.Cells(ws.Rows.Count, "B").End(xlUp).Row + 1

ws.Range("B" & lastrow).Value = Range("B2").Value
ws.Range("C" & lastrow).Value = Range("F2").Value
ws.Range("F" & lastrow).Value = Range("C2").Value
ws.Range("J" & lastrow).Value = Range("E2").Value

Step 9: Use the following code to open the text document and paste the values:

Dim FilePath As String
Dim FileContents As String

FilePath = "G:\Shared drives\Swan School Site Premises\PREMISES MANAGEMENT\SERVICE PROVIDERS\Request0.txt"

Open FilePath For Append As #1
FileContents = Range("A2") & vbNewLine & Range("B2") & vbNewLine & Range("C2") & vbNewLine & Range("D2") & vbNewLine & Range("E2") & vbNewLine
Print #1, FileContents
Close #1

Note: While testing the code, change the file path to a location where you have permissions to create files. Also, make sure to enable Trust center settings for code execution in Excel options.

The formula =IF(A2<>“”,VLOOKUP(A2,Providers!C:G,5,FALSE),“”) does not work. I want a formula that will search all Cells A2 to A100 in column C of Sheet Providers for what matches the value in Cell A2 of Sheet Job Request and from the same row where the match is found use the value in column D to populate I2 in the Sheet Job Request

To search for a match in column C of Sheet Providers from cells A2 to A100, you can use the following formula in I2 of the Sheet Job Request:

=IF(A2<>"",VLOOKUP(A2,Providers!$C$2:$D$100,2,FALSE),"")

This formula searches the range C2:D100 in the Sheet Providers for a match with the value in cell A2 of the Sheet Job Request. If a match is found, it returns the corresponding value from the same row in column D of the Sheet Providers. If a match is not found, it returns an empty string (""). 

Note that the range of cells being searched is specified as $C$2:$D$100, assuming that there are no more than 100 rows of data in Sheet Providers. You can adjust the range as needed to include all of the cells containing your data.
=VLOOKUP(A2,Providers!C3:G80,5,FALSE) can this be converted to Index Match

Yes, you can use the INDEX MATCH combination to achieve the same result as VLOOKUP. 

The equivalent of `=VLOOKUP(A2,Providers!C3:G80,5,FALSE)` using INDEX MATCH is:

`=INDEX(Providers!G$3:G$80,MATCH(A2,Providers!C$3:C$80,0))`

This formula searches for the value in A2 in column C of the range C3:C80 of Sheet Providers. It returns the corresponding value from column G of the same row where the value is found. 

Note that `MATCH(A2,Providers!C$3:C$80,0)` returns the row number of the match, and that row number is used as the index number in the `INDEX` formula to return the correct value from column G. 

Also, note that you can adjust the range `C3:C80` and `G3:G80` as per your actual data range.

If you are getting an error with the formula, there could be several reasons for that. Here are some common errors and their solutions:

#REF! error:
If you get a `#REF!` error, check if the sheet name (`Providers`) and the range address (`C$3:C$80` and `G$3:G$80`) are correctly spelled and refer to the correct range of cells where your data is located. Make sure that the range of cells you're referencing does not contain any error values or invalid characters.

#N/A error:
If the formula returns `#N/A` error, it means that it could not find a match for the value in cell A2 in the range `C3:C80`. Double-check that the value in A2 matches exactly with a value in column C of your data table. You can also try increasing the range to include more cells if necessary.

To help you further, if you could provide more details about the error message you're getting or provide a screenshot of the error along with your data, we can provide more specific solutions to your error.

Private Sub Worksheet_SelectionChange(ByVal Target As Range)
If ActiveCell.Column = 6 And Range("A2") = "" Or Range("B2") = "" Or Range("C2") = "" Or Range("D2") = "" Or Range("E2") = "" Then
MsgBox "Please fill in all the required fields.", vbExclamation, "Missing Information"
Exit Sub
End If

Dim ws As Worksheet
Set ws = Worksheets(Range(“I2”).Value)
ws.Select

Dim lastrow As Long
lastrow = ws.Cells(ws.Rows.Count, “B”).End(xlUp).Row + 1
ws.Range("B" & lastrow).Value = Range("B2").Value
ws.Range("C" & lastrow).Value = Range("F2").Value
ws.Range("F" & lastrow).Value = Range("C2").Value
ws.Range("J" & lastrow).Value = Range("E2").Value

Dim FilePath As String
Dim FileContents As String
FilePath = "G:\Shared drives\Swan School Site Premises\PREMISES MANAGEMENT\SERVICE PROVIDERS\Request0.txt"
Open FilePath For Append As #1
FileContents = Range(“A2”) & vbNewLine & Range(“B2”) & vbNewLine & Range(“C2”) & vbNewLine & Range(“D2”) & vbNewLine & Range(“E2”) & vbNewLine
Print #1, FileContents
Close #1

End Sub   correct the code that triggers the message so that after entering a value in F2 the rest of the code will run if A2 B2 C2 D2 E2 are not blank

To trigger the message after entering a value in F2, and run the remaining code if cells A2, B2, C2, D2, and E2 are not blank, you can modify the Worksheet_SelectionChange event macro as follows:

Private Sub Worksheet_SelectionChange(ByVal Target As Range)

If Target.Column = 6 And Range("A2") <> "" And Range("B2") <> "" And Range("C2") <> "" And Range("D2") <> "" And Range("E2") <> "" Then

Dim ws As Worksheet
Set ws = Worksheets(Range("I2").Value)
ws.Select

Dim lastrow As Long
lastrow = ws.Cells(ws.Rows.Count, "B").End(xlUp).Row + 1

ws.Range("B" & lastrow).Value = Range("B2").Value
ws.Range("C" & lastrow).Value = Range("F2").Value
ws.Range("F" & lastrow).Value = Range("C2").Value
ws.Range("J" & lastrow).Value = Range("E2").Value

Dim FilePath As String
Dim FileContents As String
FilePath = "G:\Shared drives\Swan School Site Premises\PREMISES MANAGEMENT\SERVICE PROVIDERS\Request0.txt"
Open FilePath For Append As #1
FileContents = Range("A2") & vbNewLine & Range("B2") & vbNewLine & Range("C2") & vbNewLine & Range("D2") & vbNewLine & Range("E2") & vbNewLine
Print #1, FileContents
Close #1

ElseIf Target.Column = 6 And (Range("A2") = "" Or Range("B2") = "" Or Range("C2") = "" Or Range("D2") = "" Or Range("E2") = "") Then
MsgBox "Please fill in all the required fields.", vbExclamation, "Missing Information"
Exit Sub

End If
End Sub

In this modified code, the first 'IF' statement runs the remaining code only if the target cell is F2 and cells A2, B2, C2, D2 and E2 are not blank. The second 'ELSEIF' statement triggers the message if the target cell is F2 and if any of these cells are blank.

Make sure to copy and paste the revised code in the worksheet module of the Sheet Job Request where you want to trigger the message.

Please correct this code Dim ws As Worksheet
Dim wss As Worksheet
Set ws = Worksheets.Sheet("Job Request")
Set wss = Worksheets(Range("I2").Value)

To correct the code, you should replace the `.Sheet("Job Request")` with just the sheet name `("Job Request")`, like this:

```
Dim ws As Worksheet
Dim wss As Worksheet
Set ws = Worksheets("Job Request")
Set wss = Worksheets(Range("I2").Value)
```

This code defines two worksheet objects `ws` and `wss`, sets `ws` to the sheet "Job Request", and then sets `wss` to the sheet specified in cell I2 of the "Job Request" sheet. 

Make sure that the sheet name in cell I2 is spelled exactly as the name of one of the worksheets in your workbook, otherwise you may get a run-time error '9': Subscript out of range.

Sub Request()
Dim ws As Worksheet
Dim wss As Worksheet
Set ws = Worksheets("Job Request")
Set wss = Worksheets(Range("I2").Value)

wss.Select

Dim lastrow As Long
lastrow = ws.Cells(ws.Rows.Count, "B").End(xlUp).Row + 1
wss.Range("B" & lastrow).Value = ws.Range("B2").Value
wss.Range("C" & lastrow).Value = ws.Range("F2").Value
wss.Range("F" & lastrow).Value = ws.Range("C2").Value
wss.Range("J" & lastrow).Value = ws.Range("E2").Value

ws.Select

Dim FilePath As String
Dim FileContents As String
FilePath = "G:\Shared drives\Swan School Site Premises\PREMISES MANAGEMENT\SERVICE PROVIDERS\Request0.txt"
Open FilePath For Append As #1
FileContents = Range("A2") & vbNewLine & Range("B2") & vbNewLine & Range("C2") & vbNewLine & Range("D2") & vbNewLine & Range("E2") & vbNewLine
Print #1, FileContents
Close #1

ElseIf Target.Column = 6 And Range("A2") = "" Or Range("B2") = "" Or Range("C2") = "" Or Range("D2") = "" Or Range("E2") = "" Then
MsgBox "Please fill in all the required fields.", vbExclamation, "Missing Information"""
Exit Sub
End If
End If
End Sub   there is an error with the elseif

Based on the code you have provided, it seems that there is an error in the `ElseIf` statement. 

Here is the corrected code for `Request()` macro:

```
Sub Request()
    Dim ws As Worksheet
    Dim wss As Worksheet
    
    If Range("A2") = "" Or Range("B2") = "" Or Range("C2") = "" Or Range("D2") = "" Or Range("E2") = "" Then
        MsgBox "Please fill in all the required fields.", vbExclamation, "Missing Information"
        Exit Sub
    End If
    
    Set ws = Worksheets("Job Request")
    Set wss = Worksheets(Range("I2").Value)
    
    wss.Select
    
    Dim lastrow As Long
    lastrow = wss.Cells(wss.Rows.Count, "B").End(xlUp).Row + 1
    wss.Range("B" & lastrow).Value = ws.Range("B2").Value
    wss.Range("C" & lastrow).Value = ws.Range("F2").Value
    wss.Range("F" & lastrow).Value = ws.Range("C2").Value
    wss.Range("J" & lastrow).Value = ws.Range("E2").Value
    
    ws.Select
    
    Dim FilePath As String
    Dim FileContents As String
    
    FilePath = "G:\Shared drives\Swan School Site Premises\PREMISES MANAGEMENT\SERVICE PROVIDERS\Request0.txt"
    Open FilePath For Append As #1
    FileContents = Range("A2") & vbNewLine & Range("B2") & vbNewLine & Range("C2") & vbNewLine & Range("D2") & vbNewLine & Range("E2") & vbNewLine
    Print #1, FileContents
    Close #1
End Sub
```

In this code, the `ElseIf` statement has been removed since it was causing the error. Instead, the code checks for missing information before proceeding with the rest of the macro. 

After checking for missing information, the code sets the worksheet objects `ws` and `wss` to the appropriate sheets, copies the data from the Job Request sheet to the Service Providers sheet, and saves the values in the text file specified by `FilePath`. 

Make sure to copy and paste the revised code in a standard module of your workbook.

Dim FilePath As String
Dim FileContents As String
FilePath = "G:\Shared drives\Swan School Site Premises\PREMISES MANAGEMENT\SERVICE PROVIDERS\Request0.txt"
Open FilePath For Append As #1
FileContents = Range("A2") & vbNewLine & Range("B2") & vbNewLine & Range("C2") & vbNewLine & Range("D2") & vbNewLine & Range("E2") & vbNewLine
Print #1, FileContents
Close #1    does not work

The code you have provided will not work because it appears outside of any VBA procedures (Sub or Function). You should put it into a subroutine or into an existing subroutine in order to run it.

Here is what the full subroutine should look like:

```
Sub WriteToFile()
    Dim FilePath As String
    Dim FileContents As String
    
    FilePath = "G:\Shared drives\Swan School Site Premises\PREMISES MANAGEMENT\SERVICE PROVIDERS\Request0.txt"
    
    FileContents = Range("A2") & vb
